concerns.text: T030 (Correlation Analysis) is listed as a parallel task alongside T029/T029b (DFT parsing), but the 'Critical Execution Order' section explicitly states T030 consumes T029/T029b outputs. The [P] tag on T030 is incorrect as it has a hard data dependency on the completion of T029/T029b. T030 must be moved to a sequential position after T029b.

T029b (Parse explicit solvent results) is marked [P] (parallel-safe), but its description explicitly states it 'depends on T029c'. A task cannot be parallel-safe if it has a hard dependency on another task within the same phase. The [P] tag on T029b is invalid.

T012 (Integration test for US1) is marked [P] but the description states it 'depends on T014'. The 'Parallel Opportunities' section also notes T012 cannot run in parallel with T014. The [P] tag on T012 is incorrect and violates the producer-consumer ordering.

T039 (Full pipeline integration test) is marked [P] but the description states it 'depends on T026, T034, AND completion of Phase 4 and Phase 5'. A task that depends on the completion of entire phases cannot be parallel-safe. The [P] tag on T039 is invalid.

T016 is tagged as [US1] but implements `code/analysis/calibration.py` which is functionally required for US2 (FR-004) to extract lifetimes. While not a hard ordering violation, the user story mapping creates a semantic dependency confusion where US2 implementation relies on a task grouped under US1, potentially breaking the 'independently-shippable unit' requirement for US1 if US2 is not yet started.
